rationale.R1.2: Pervasive tense inconsistencies ('evaluated'/'will use'/'we found'/'will reduce' in the same passage) fracture the logical chain; the explicit admission that closed-loop stability was assumed rather than proven is the single largest logical liability and must be addressed or clearly scoped for the passage to hold up as a technical argument.
rationale.R1.3: The headline result (23% reduction in integrated tracking error) appears without confidence intervals, variance across the 12-plant benchmark, or baseline specification; 'relatively robust to measurement noise' and 'responds quickly' are qualitative and unsupported — strengthening requires grounding these claims in the evidence the passage already implies exists.
rationale.R1.6: Run-on sentences throughout, mixed past/future tense, weak hedges ('appears to be', 'we believe', 'we had considered'), and parenthetical self-references ('which we had previously studied in earlier work') are the primary surface failure; writing polish is the load-bearing dimension for any 'make this stronger' delegation on academic prose.
